response.justification: Exceptional full-stack engineer with 18+ years of production experience across web, mobile, embedded systems, and now AI agent infrastructure. Exceeds all core qualifications and required technical skills except for explicit Nest.js and Vitest framework mention, both easily learnable given demonstrated TypeScript/Node.js mastery. Current role explicitly includes AI-agent development and MCP frameworks—a rare, specific requirement. Deductions only for framework-specific gaps, not fundamental capability.
strengths: AI/ML agent infrastructure expertise with hands-on MCP framework development—a specific required skill few candidates possess
18+ years shipping to production across diverse domains (millions of mails/second at Optilyz, top-10 iOS app at WatchUp, real-time systems at Dunnhumby)

Genuine full-stack mastery demonstrated across multiple roles: backend (Node, TypeScript, DLang, Java), frontend (React, Ember.js), databases (MongoDB, PostgreSQL, MySQL), cloud (AWS CDK, Docker, Kubernetes)
concerns: Nest.js not mentioned on resume despite being primary stack requirement—framework-specific learning needed, though Node.js/TypeScript fundamentals are strong
Vitest expertise not evidenced; only generic TDD/testing background shown (workshops, fluent-asserts library for D language)
Recent pivot from product engineering (Optilyz) to infrastructure/tooling (re:cinq) may require re-acclimation to feature-level delivery cadence and product collaboration
